answer: Budget Summary Dashboard, Category Breakdown Donut Chart, Spending Insights Period Summary, Daily Spending Chart (Bar/Line Toggle), Month Navigation, Top Navigation Bar, Export CSV, Empty & Error States, Responsive Layout Breakpoints, Reduced Motion Support, Keyboard Navigation & Accessibility
Just the essentials — id and amount. Fast to enter, minimal effort.
The app ships with 6 preset categories (Food, Transport, Shopping, etc.) and users cannot add, rename, or remove them.
All expenses are saved in the browser on the current device. No sign-up or login required — open the app and start tracking.
The app notices which expenses you log most often and shows them as one-tap shortcuts at the bottom of the Add Expense screen. No manual setup required.
selectedOption: Smart suggestions based on past entries

answer: A button on the Insights screen lets users download all their expenses as a spreadsheet file for use in Excel or Google Sheets.
Include all charts: daily spending (bar and line toggle), category breakdown with percentages, top 5 biggest expenses, and a this-month vs last-month comparison.
